overview.short: Asurgarh is an archaeological site in the Kalahandi district of Odisha, India.
overview.description: Asurgarh is an archaeological site in the Kalahandi district of Odisha, India. Asurgarh is one of the sites which has its beginning in around 5th century BCE to 5th century AD and emerged as one of the early urban fortified settlements in the region and it is older than Sisupalgarh. Pre-Mauryan, Mauryan and Gupta period coins are found in large number, these coins, discovered at Asurgarh, are categorized into two groups: 69 belong to the pre-Mauryan period, while 272 are from the Mauryan epoch and Gupta period. Excavations at Asurgarh in the Kalahandi district of Odisha have provided evidence

topPlaces.name: Narla
topPlaces.description: Narla is a town in Narla Tehsil in Kalahandi district in the Indian state of Odisha. This town is maintaining 29 km distance from its district main city Bhawanipatna and 430 km far from its state capital Bhubaneswar.

items.note: Narla is a town in Narla Tehsil in Kalahandi district in the Indian state of Odisha. This town is maintaining 29 km distance from its district main city Bhawanipatna and 430 km far from its state capital Bhubaneswar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Asurgarh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Asurgarh is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (206 km) and the nearest railway station is Sambalpur Junction (166 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Asurgarh?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Asurgarh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Asurgarh?
faq.a: Stays in Asurgarh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
